title: Proof David Bullard is Lying About SB13 to Pastors and Oklahomans
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States documents that Oklahoma State Senator David Bullard has been telling pastors that SB13's author refused to make his requested edits, when in fact those edits were filed in committee substitute Req. No. 1933 and Bullard was personally handed the updated version. The article cites Senator Joseph Silk's statement and shows the same false talking point being repeated by Senator Larry Boggs and former Pittsburg County GOP Chair Lonnie Lu Anderson.
audiences: Oklahoma pastor who met with Bullard
pro-life voter weighing SB13 vs. SB1728
journalist fact-checking the SB13 amendment claim

questions.question: What two-pronged campaign is Senator David Bullard running between election cycles?
questions.answer: Bullard is working both to suppress support for SB13 (The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act) and to drum up support for his own bill, SB1728, by traveling Oklahoma to meet with abolitionist and pro-life pastors and by appearing at campaign events.
questions.quote: His current campaign is to (1) decrease support for The Abolition of Abortion in Oklahoma Act (SB13) and (2) increase support for his bill, SB1728. He has been traveling the state to meet with a number of abolitionist and pro-life pastors and has spoken at at least one campaign event for another State Senator.

questions.question: What is Bullard's stated reason for opposing SB13, and what evidence does he point to?
questions.audience: Oklahoma pastor who met with Bullard
questions.answer: Bullard claims Senator Joseph Silk never made the edits Bullard requested, and he points pastors to the Oklahoma state legislature's website, which still shows the original SB13 text without his amendments.
questions.quote: Bullard's present argument against SB13 is that Silk did not make the edits Bullard requested. In meetings, Bullard has been pointing to the state website to defend his claim. The state website still shows the original SB13 without his requested edits.

questions.question: Why doesn't the amended version of SB13 appear on the state website?
questions.audience: journalist fact-checking the SB13 amendment claim
questions.answer: The committee substitute incorporating Bullard's edits never received a committee hearing, so no legislative action was taken that would push the new text onto the state website. Senator Greg McCortney denied SB13 a hearing.
questions.quote: What Bullard is not telling pastors is that the version of SB13 on the state website does not contain the edits because the bill never received a committee hearing after the committee substitute was filed. Without a committee hearing, there was never a chance for the edits to be reflected on the website.

questions.question: How can Oklahomans actually verify that SB13 was amended with Bullard's requested changes?
questions.audience: journalist fact-checking the SB13 amendment claim
questions.answer: Every filed bill or committee substitute carries a unique request number printed on the bottom-left of each page. The original SB13 carries Req. No. 62, while the committee substitute containing Bullard's edits carries Req. No. 1933, providing a paper trail independent of the state website.
questions.quote: Every piece of legislation, including committee substitutes, comes with a unique request number when it is officially filed. Request numbers appear on the bottom left corner of each page of the bill. The request number for the [original SB13](https://www.legiscan.com/OK/text/SB13/2019) is 62. The request number for the [committee substitute](https://freethestates.org/wp-content/uploads/2020/04/SB13-Updated-Language.pdf) with Bullard's requested edits is 1933.

questions.question: What proof does the article offer that Bullard knew his requested edits had been incorporated?
questions.answer: Senator Joseph Silk states that during a February 2019 meeting in his office he personally handed Bullard the new version of SB13 with Bullard's suggested edits highlighted, and Bullard approved them before later voting against the bill.
questions.quote: In particular, in February of 2019 I personally handed Senator David Bullard the updated version of SB13 with the changes highlighted, and he approved.  He then fell in line with corrupt Senate Leadership, voted against the bill and told the public that the changes he requested were not made even though there is documented proof to the contrary.

questions.question: Does Senator Silk think these legislators have any cover for not amending SB13 themselves?
questions.answer: No. Silk argues that legislators who claim they would have supported SB13 if changes had been made are either lying or incompetent, because legislators have the power to amend any bill themselves rather than waiting on the author.
questions.quote: Additionally, even if the changes had not been made they as legislators have the power to amend any bill themselves.  The excuses continue year after year and are getting weaker and more untrue every year.

questions.question: What happened when a pastor confronted Bullard directly with the two request numbers?
questions.audience: Oklahoma pastor who met with Bullard
questions.answer: Bullard acted confused when shown Req. No. 1933, promised to get back to the pastor with an explanation, and then went silent for over a week, which Free the States interprets as confirmation that the discrepancy is unexplainable.
questions.quote: A pastor who had met with Bullard, and had been lied to by Bullard, reached out to him for an explanation of Req. No. 1933. Bullard acted confused when he was confronted with the two Req. Nos. and said he would try to get back to the pastor with an explanation. A week has gone by since that conversation and Bullard still has not gotten back to the pastor.

questions.question: Which other Oklahoma senator is publicly repeating Bullard's false talking point, and where?
questions.answer: Senator Larry Boggs ran a campaign ad in The Weekly Bargain Journal echoing Bullard's claim that SB13 was never amended, dismissing the committee substitute as a 'fake document' and pointing readers to the official state bill page that shows 'no amendment.'
questions.quote: "Despite what you may be told or fake document you've been shown, the bill was never amended," Boggs wrote. "Here is a link to show the bill's official progress – note 'no amendment' – that means the author never changed the SB13 bill. I've always shot straight with you."

questions.question: Did Senator Silk also brief Boggs personally on the amended language?
questions.audience: journalist fact-checking the SB13 amendment claim
questions.answer: Yes. Silk says he spoke with Boggs early in the session about the changes that were made and that Boggs acknowledged understanding them, meaning Boggs's later public denial is, by Silk's account, a knowing lie to retain his seat.
questions.quote: "Sadly, Senator Larry Boggs is knowingly lying about SB13 in order to try to retain his seat," Silk said. "I personally spoke with Boggs early this year during session and informed him of changes that were made, and he acknowledged his understanding. The Republican establishment have been backed into a corner on this issue and are doing everything they can to save face, even spreading totally false information."

questions.question: How is the false narrative spreading beyond the state senate itself?
questions.answer: Establishment allies outside the legislature, such as former Pittsburg County GOP Chair Lonnie Lu Anderson, are amplifying the same misinformation, showing how quickly disinformation propagates once a sitting senator gives it cover.
questions.quote: Tragically, when those in positions of influence – such as a State Senator – decide to spread lies, the disinformation spreads quickly. Allies of the Republican establishment such as former Pittsburg County GOP Chair Lonnie Lu Anderson have been spreading the misinformation as well.

questions.question: What does Free the States ask readers to do in response?
questions.audience: pro-life voter weighing SB13 vs. SB1728
questions.answer: Because the lie is easily disprovable, Free the States asks readers to out-circulate the lie by sharing the article with Oklahoma friends and family, denying the establishment a path to escape accountability for blocking abortion's abolition.
questions.quote: With a lie that is so easily disprovable, they appear to be betting on their hope that they can reach more people with the lie than we can reach with the truth. Don't let that be the case. Don't let the establishment lie their way out of the just outrage against them for their failure to abolish abortion. Share this article with Oklahoma friends and family.

questions.question: How does Silk frame the moral weight of these legislators' behavior?
questions.audience: Oklahoma pastor who met with Bullard
questions.answer: Silk frames it as professing Christians engaging in hypocritical, corrupt politics whose practical effect is the continued killing of innocent children, calling it some of the worst lying and evil he has personally witnessed.
questions.quote: Never in my life have I witnessed more outright lies and evilness in people who profess to be followers of Christ. This is hypocritical, corrupt politics at it's worst.  All at the expense of innocent children continuing to be murdered.

related.title: Anti-SB13 Campaigners Aren't Ignorant. They're Trying to Deceive You.
related.reason: Establishes the same thesis this article applies to Bullard: opposition to SB13 is rooted in deliberate deception, not honest disagreement.

related.title: Senator McCortney Again Denies SB13 a Fair Hearing
related.reason: Explains the procedural blockade by Senator Greg McCortney that this article cites as the reason the amended SB13 never appeared on the state website.

related.title: Free the States Memo to OK Senators Regarding SB1728
related.reason: Lays out why Bullard's competing bill SB1728, which he is promoting alongside attacking SB13, is rejected by abolitionists.

related.title: Remember Bullard's Claim That "thousands of unborn babies will be saved every year" by SB1728
related.reason: A companion piece holding Bullard accountable for separate misleading claims about SB1728, the bill he was elevating in place of SB13.

related.title: Watch - Of Giants & Heroes: Joseph Silk
related.reason: Profiles Joseph Silk, the SB13 author whose firsthand statement is the load-bearing evidence in this article.

related.title: Responding to Larry Boggs' Explanation of Tuesday's Confrontation
related.reason: Continues the accountability thread on Senator Larry Boggs, who is shown here echoing Bullard's false 'no amendment' talking point.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Defeats Larry Boggs, Margie Alfonso Defeats Clay Iiams
related.reason: Reports the eventual primary outcome for Boggs, one of the senators identified in this article as knowingly spreading the SB13 lie.
